continuity_intent: Resume supercell automation-runner work from the latest machine-auditable execution-record layer without re-deriving prior layers.

current_baseline.current_state: Runner execution records now expose structured input_checks, pointer_checks, phase_results, and overall status.
current_baseline.latest_focus: Make the execution record more machine-auditable and preserve continuity across an overlong chat.

objectives_in_progress: Operationalize the ATTICUS supercell as a cross-model authoritative memory repo.
Turn the supercell runner from a documentation-only scaffold into a minimally testable local runner.
Keep current pointers, schemas, examples, and refresh logs synchronized after each layer addition.

completed_layers.result: Canonical refresh protocol, receipt template, refresh log, project registry, lifecycle ledgers, memory-cell index, and resume-packet index established.
Machine-readable refresh manifest schema and concrete current refresh-runner input created.
Generator spec, input schema, input example, output schema, and current generated session-state bundle created.
Supercell validation protocol, validation report schema, and current validation report created.
Supersession/archival policy plus artifact lifecycle status schema and current registry created.
Stub spec, runner config schema, current config, execution-record schema, and execution-record examples created.
Concrete runtime files created under tools/runner/: README.md, main.py, phases/__init__.py, phases/refresh.py, phases/generate.py, phases/validate.py.
Runner now performs live required-input and key-pointer existence checks and writes to docs/AUTOMATION_RUNNER_EXECUTION_RECORD.generated.json.
Phase hooks now return pass/warn/fail from actual loaded content; README includes real local run commands.
Execution record schema and runner output now include structured input_checks and pointer_checks in addition to phase_results.

phase_hooks.refresh: Fails on missing required refresh-runner keys; warns on empty critical lists; passes on structurally complete input.
phase_hooks.generate: Fails on missing required generator keys; warns on missing/invalid/tight bounds; passes on structurally complete bounded input.
phase_hooks.validate: Fails on missing/invalid validation report shape; warns when expected domains are missing or report status is warn; passes on complete healthy validation report.

current_limits: Runner does not yet perform real schema validation against JSON Schema.
Runner does not yet execute real refresh/generation/validation mutations.
Generated execution-record artifact in repo is still a seeded baseline example, not proof of a live repo-side run from this chat.

next_recommended_step.recommendation: Add runtime-emission receipt fields to the execution record.
next_recommended_step.why: The next clean hardening step is to make each generated execution record self-describing as a runtime artifact, not just a status snapshot.

use_when_starting_next_chat: State that work should resume from ATTICUS supercell runner audit-detail layer.
Preserve current_refresh_id = refresh-runner-audit-detail-layer-2026-04-17 unless superseded by newer repo state.
Do not regress to pre-audit-detail execution-record shape.
Next action: extend execution record with runtime-emission receipt fields and refresh CURRENT_REFRESH.json and STATE_REFRESH_LOG.json after the change.
rehydration_instructions.operator_prompt_seed: Resume ATTICUS supercell work from the runner-audit-detail layer. Current runner supports structured input_checks, pointer_checks, phase_results, and overall status. Next hardening step: add emitted_at, runner_version, repo_root, and output_path to the execution record, then refresh CURRENT_REFRESH.json and STATE_REFRESH_LOG.json.

notes: This cell is a continuity artifact for cross-chat resumption because the source chat became too long.
It is intended to preserve the relevant operational state, not to replace the repo’s canonical docs.
Use this cell together with the repo’s CURRENT_REFRESH.json when resuming.
